Event: He starts the series with a crewcut
Explanation: Neutral-positive grooming choice with no indicated harm and mild social/professional benefit.

Event: He starts growing his hair out in Season 6
Explanation: Personal style change with no shown downside and some implied confidence/appeal.

Event: He grew from a crewcut to a fuller mane and beard
Explanation: His fuller hair and beard are associated with attractiveness and no negative effects.

Event: He later put on some weight with a visible belly
Explanation: Weight gain modestly harms his health/self while not hurting and even appealing to others.

Event: He is found sexy despite weight gain
Explanation: Others’ attraction benefits him socially/romantically without harming anyone.

Event: He really took it hard when cousin Robbie died in Vietnam
Explanation: His deep grief harms himself emotionally but reflects caring for another.

Event: Much of his obnoxious behavior is tolerated because he's quite good at his job
Explanation: His competence benefits him while his tolerated obnoxiousness makes others’ work life worse.

Event: He goes from being chauvinistic and full of himself to being understanding and caring
Explanation: Personal growth improves his own relationships and clearly benefits colleagues.

Event: He gets together with Peggy
Explanation: Mutual romantic partnership benefits both with no indicated downside.

Event: Stan encourages Peggy to go to Paris with Mathis's brother‑in‑law
Explanation: He sacrifices his own romantic prospects to help Peggy loosen up and be happy.

Event: Peggy challenges him to put his money where his mouth is and work nude
Explanation: Her provocation tests him and work commitment but creates an awkward, risky situation.

Event: Stan works alongside Peggy in the nude
Explanation: He gains no clear benefit and increases professional and personal risk/awkwardness for both.

Event: He has others try to hit a paper apple above his head with their pens while high on a booster
Explanation: His drug‑fueled game entertains him but endangers others and disrupts work.

Event: Michael tries and ends up hitting Stan’s arm with a craft razor
Explanation: Accidental injury harms Stan without helping Michael or anyone else.

Event: Stan responds to being cut with a mild “Aw crap”
Explanation: Staying calm minimizes panic and disruption, modestly benefiting everyone.

Event: Stan and Peggy keep in touch after she hops to Ted’s firm
Explanation: Maintaining their relationship supports both emotionally and professionally.

Event: Stan constantly smokes marijuana in the SCDP offices
Explanation: He claims creative benefit but risks professionalism and others’ comfort and workplace norms.

Event: He claims to do better work when stoned
Explanation: Justification helps him keep using at work, while sustaining a problematic norm for others.

Event: Stan pretty much keeps his wits together for the team
Explanation: Acting as “Team Dad” stabilizes the environment and gives him a solid, respected role.

Event: He becomes much more kind‑hearted and noble over time
Explanation: Kindness and nobility improve his life and significantly help those around him.

Event: Discovering pot seems to have helped his increased kindness
Explanation: In this narrative, marijuana use correlates with emotional softening that benefits him and colleagues.

Event: He is an understanding platonic then romantic partner to Peggy
Explanation: His emotional support and understanding strongly help Peggy and deepen his own fulfillment.

Event: Stan and Peggy declare their love for each other
Explanation: Mutual confession secures a relationship that clearly benefits both.
